cases.title: Scenario-matrix coverage for six-role security and scope gate on protected routes
preconditions: Role-matrix fixture provides authenticated users for Student, Lecturer, DepartmentAdmin, AcademicAdmin, ITAdmin, and SystemAuditor with known scope boundaries.
Protected resources exist: open/close session control, check-in, attendance roster, report query, export job, audit log query, and academic setup endpoints.
Attendance and session data span multiple class sections and faculties to exercise scope isolation.
steps: For each role, exercise allowed protected routes per permissions matrix §3.1–3.2 and API catalog §4.

For each role, attempt denied actions: Student on session open/close and exports; Lecturer on unassigned section control and cross-section reports; ITAdmin on academic exports without grant; SystemAuditor on mutations and exports.
Verify GET /v1/reports/attendance and POST /v1/exports/attendance enforce role scope per AC-15/AC-16 patterns.
Verify POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open and /close succeed only for Lecturer on assigned sections and AcademicAdmin override.
Verify POST /v1/check-ins is limited to Student role with enrollment scope.
cases.expected: Maps to testing-plan §7.3 security and authorization tests and §11 NFR-09 row: only authorized scope is accessible for each of the six roles; every unauthorized protected route or export operation is denied without cross-scope data leakage (AC-23, NFR-09).

cases.title: Authorization scope guard enforces deny-by-default at module boundary before data access
preconditions: Integration database seeded with role-to-scope assignments and attendance rows across multiple sections and faculties.
Scope-resolution and permission-guard modules use real DB lookups for actor role and target entity scope.
steps: Invoke report query use-case for Lecturer actor with classSectionId outside assigned set.
Invoke export job creation use-case for ITAdmin actor without academic grant.
Invoke session open use-case for Student actor on a valid scheduled session.
Verify scope guard short-circuits each call before unrestricted repository queries execute.
Assert zero rows from foreign scope are read, persisted, or returned.
cases.expected: Module integration rejects all three operations at authorization boundary with deny-by-default semantics (RBAC-01, RBAC-02, PRM-10); no cross-scope attendance or session data is accessed.

cases.title: HTTP contract for unauthenticated access to protected route returns 401
preconditions: Valid class session and attendance data exist in the database.
No Authorization header is supplied on the request.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance without Bearer token.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope per API §2.3.
Inspect data field and error.code value.
cases.expected: Response is 401 with standard { data: null, meta, error } envelope; error.code is Unauthenticated; data is null and no attendance rows or scope metadata are disclosed.

cases.title: HTTP contract for assigned Lecturer authorized on POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open
preconditions: Scheduled class session exists in a section assigned to the authenticated Lecturer.
Lecturer bearer token is valid.
steps: Send POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open with Lecturer Authorization header and required Idempotency-Key.
Validate HTTP status code and JSON response envelope.
Verify data.state is Open and data.openedAt is present per API §4.3.
cases.expected: Response is 200 with standard envelope; data contains classSessionId, state Open, openedAt timestamp, and qr preview metadata; authorized Lecturer scope is confirmed (positive security path).

cases.title: Lecturer session open and close on assigned section; Student denied session control
preconditions: Class session exists in Scheduled state for a section assigned to Lecturer L.
Student S is enrolled in the section; Student bearer token is available.
Integration environment uses real session state machine persistence.
steps: POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as Lecturer L; verify state transitions Scheduled to Open.
POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as Lecturer L; verify state transitions Open to Closed.
POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open as Student S on a different scheduled session.
POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/close as Student S.
cases.expected: Lecturer open and close succeed with persisted state transitions (AC-01, AC-05); Student session control attempts are rejected with 403 Forbidden and session state remains unchanged.

cases.title: Student denied SessionControl.execute on open and close endpoints
preconditions: Student is authenticated with valid Bearer token.
Scheduled and Open class sessions exist with enrollment for the student.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{scheduledSessionId}/open as Student.
Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{openSessionId}/close as Student.
cases.expected: Both requests return 403 with Forbidden or OutOfScope error.code; no session state mutation occurs and responses disclose no foreign-section session metadata (permissions matrix SessionControl.execute Deny for Student).

cases.title: Student denied institution-wide report query and export execution
preconditions: Student is authenticated.
Institution-wide attendance data exists across multiple sections.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId} as Student.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with format csv and term-wide filters as Student.
cases.expected: Both requests return 403 Forbidden; responses contain no attendance rows, pagination totals, exportJobId, or other restricted academic data (ReportView.read and ExportJob.execute Deny for Student).

cases.title: ITAdmin denied academic report and export without explicit elevated grant
preconditions: ITAdmin is authenticated without explicit academic access grant.
Attendance data exists across institution scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId} as ITAdmin.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with term-wide csv filters as ITAdmin.
Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} as ITAdmin.
cases.expected: All three requests return 403 Forbidden; no attendance rows, export jobs, or attendance mutations are produced (permissions matrix §5.5 deny-by-default for academic mutations).

cases.title: SystemAuditor denied attendance mutations and export while read-only scope applies
preconditions: SystemAuditor is authenticated with scoped read-only attendance access.
Open session with attendance records exists within auditor read scope.
steps: Call GET /v1/reports/attendance within auditor read scope and confirm allowed read behavior.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters matching auditor read scope.
Call PATCH /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance/{studentUserId} with status Manual Present as SystemAuditor.
cases.expected: Scoped report read may succeed per role policy, but export and attendance PATCH return 403 Forbidden with no exportJobId and no attendance mutation persisted (PRM-05, SystemAuditor read-only).

cases.title: Lecturer denied cross-section session control, report, and export for unassigned classSectionId
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned only to section A.
Section B has a Scheduled session and attendance data; section B is not in lecturer assignment set.
steps: Call POST /v1/class-sessions/{sectionBSessionId}/open as lecturer assigned to section A only.
Call GET /v1/reports/attendance?classSectionId={sectionBId} as lecturer.
Call POST /v1/exports/attendance with filters { classSectionId: sectionBId } as lecturer.
cases.expected: All three requests return 403 with OutOfScope or Forbidden semantics; responses contain no partial section B rows, session state changes, attendance statuses, or export job identifiers (PRM-01, PRM-10).

cases.title: Non-Student roles denied POST /v1/check-ins while Student role is authorized
preconditions: Open class session with valid QR token exists; enrolled Student has no prior success.
Lecturer and AcademicAdmin bearer tokens are available.
steps: Submit POST /v1/check-ins with valid qrToken as enrolled Student; verify 200 Success.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with same valid qrToken as Lecturer.
Submit POST /v1/check-ins with same valid qrToken as AcademicAdmin.
cases.expected: Student check-in succeeds with outcome Success; Lecturer and AcademicAdmin attempts return 403 Forbidden with no attendance record created for non-student actors (CheckInAttempt role restriction, positive Student-only path).

cases.title: Denied authorization responses do not leak cross-scope pagination or entity metadata
preconditions: Institution has 500+ attendance rows across sections; unauthorized actor lacks scope for requested filters.
Unauthorized Student or cross-scope Lecturer is authenticated.
steps: Send GET /v1/reports/attendance?termId={termId}&page=1&pageSize=25 as unauthorized actor.
Inspect error response body, meta.pagination fields, and error.details if present.
Send POST /v1/exports/attendance with foreign classSectionId filter as scoped lecturer.
Compare institution-wide totals against any values returned in denial responses.
cases.expected: All denials return 403 without meta.pagination.totalItems or totalPages reflecting institution-wide or foreign-scope counts; error.details contains no student names, attendance statuses, or section row samples (API §2.5, CMP-04).

cases.title: Authorized export mutation produces immutable audit record per protected mutation path
preconditions: Lecturer is authenticated and assigned to target class section with attendance data.
Audit log query is available to AcademicAdmin or SystemAuditor.
steps: Submit POST /v1/exports/attendance with scoped csv filters as authorized Lecturer.
Verify export job is created with 202 response.
Query GET /v1/audit-logs filtered by actorUserId and export action type.
Verify audit entry fields: actor, role, scope filter, file format, timestamp.
cases.expected: Successful scoped export writes immutable audit log entry with required fields per NFR-10 and permissions §7.1; unauthorized actors cannot trigger equivalent audit-covered mutations.

cases.title: Primary authorized Lecturer workflow: open session, view scoped roster, export in scope
preconditions: Lecturer is assigned to section SE101-01 with scheduled session and enrolled students.
Attendance data exists in SE101-01 and unassigned section SE101-02.
steps: Authenticate as lecturer; POST /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/open.
GET /v1/class-sessions/{sessionId}/attendance and verify roster rows are section-scoped.
POST /v1/exports/attendance with csv format and term filters covering assigned section only.
Verify export output contains only SE101-01 rows.
cases.expected: End-to-end authorized workflow completes without denial; all data accessed and exported remains within lecturer assigned scope with no SE101-02 leakage (positive security gate per AC-23).

cases.title: Student browser journey blocked from session control, report, and export surfaces
preconditions: Student is logged in on mobile or desktop browser.
Application shell exposes navigation for lecturer and admin features.
Institution attendance data exists but Student role lacks SessionControl, ReportView, and ExportJob permissions.
steps: Observe main navigation menu for session open/close, report, and export links.
Attempt direct URL navigation to lecturer session control and attendance report pages.
If any admin surface is reachable, attempt to trigger export or session open action.
Verify user-facing denial or redirect messaging.
cases.expected: Student UI does not expose session control, institution report listing, or export actions; direct URL navigation shows access-denied state or redirect without displaying other students' or sections' attendance data.

cases.title: Lecturer browser journey shows scoped session control and export; denies foreign section tamper
preconditions: Lecturer is logged in on desktop browser.
Dashboard lists only assigned class sections and sessions.
Deep-link or URL tamper to foreign classSectionId is possible.
steps: Navigate to session list and confirm only assigned sections and sessions appear.
Open attendance for assigned session and trigger Open Attendance action.
Navigate to report/export page; confirm section filter lists assigned sections only.
Attempt export or roster view with URL parameter set to unassigned classSectionId.
cases.expected: UI surfaces session open/close and export only for assigned scope; tampered foreign-section requests show access-denied feedback without previewing foreign-section attendance rows or partial export download.
